comments.text: [HJZ26] by S. Huang, Y. Ju and Y. Zhou has proved this conjecture for no induced copies of $E$-graph, where $E$-graph is the graph obtained from the five-vertex path $P_5$ by adding a pendent edge to the middle vertex of the $P_5$.

Hello, I have been working on some local formulations around the EH conjecture. I am not claiming proof of EH, and I cannot guarantee all findings in the notes are new. In light of the discussion here: https://www.erdosproblems.com/forum/thread/AI%20Contributions I spent a substantial amount of time sorting through my notes, keeping what I believe to be valuable, and reviewing existing literature for relevance and novelty. These notes were developed with substantial AI assistance over many sessions, and Codex helped me formalize some of the finite statements in Lean. If anyone has time, the parts I would most appreciate scrutiny on are Theorem 2.3, Theorems 3.7 and 3.8, Propositions 5.3 and 5.6, and Theorem 6.9 1. For every \(m>4\), the singleton-cut rows \(R_m=\{0,\delta e_1,\dots,\delta e_m\}\subseteq \operatorname{im}\delta\) have ladder index \(\le 2\), but every exact affine cell in \(R_m\) has size \(\le 2\), so any exact affine-cell cover needs at least \(\lceil(m+1)/2\rceil\) cells. 2. For every \(k\ge6\), the first-splitter construction with \(S=\{q_{k-2},q_{k-1}\}\) and \(T=\{q_{k-4},q_{k-2},q_{k-1}\}\) satisfies \(G_k(R)\) is \(P_k\)-free iff the bipartite interface \(R\) is \(P_k\)-free. 3. For a prime graph \(H\) with a mixed ordering, the two-anchor \((\rho,\lambda)\)-split condition can hold at every positive scale while no positive-width contraction is rich, so two-anchor splitting does not synchronize to a transversal copy of \(H\). 4. The coordinatewise split replacement does synchronize: for fixed \(0<\rho_0<\rho_1\le1\) and \(0<\lambda'<\lambda\le1\), every sufficiently wide coordinatewise \((\rho_0,\lambda)\)-split blockade is \((\rho_1,\lambda')\)-rich. 5. For rectangle traces \(\mathcal R(M)=\{E(M)\cap(P\times Q):P\subseteq U,\ Q\subseteq V\}\), a finite set \(F\subseteq E(M)\) is shattered by \(\mathcal R(M)\) iff \((V(F),F)\) is a star forest. The link to the note: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1JVSpoaEIlJ7_btpEPDe2TkJdhg8ybDja/view?usp=sharing Lean formalizations for some of the statements:https://github.com/SamMausberg/lean-formalizations/tree/main/FormalConjectures/Problems/Erdos/E61
Thanks. Standard check found no issues on the notes.
Thanks, Nat, for running a check on this and my earlier post :)

The thesis of H. T. Nguyen is a really nice account of this conjecture. Here's what it proves: - A proof of the conjecture when $H$ is the five-vertex path. - A proof of the conjecture in the setting of graphs of bounded VC-dimension. - A proof of the conjecture for infinitely many prime graphs $H$ - A proof of the bound $2^{(\log n)^{1-(o)1}}$ when $H$ is an arbitrary path. - A proof of the conjecture for excluding a hole and an antihole, and for excluding an arbitrary induced $(≥ 4)$-subdivision of $H$ and its complement. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

This conjecture has been proven true for "nearly" all paths, i.e., for every path $H$, all $H$-free graphs with $n$ vertices have cliques or stable sets of size at least $2^{(\log n)^{1−o(1)}}$ by T. Nguyen, A. Scott and P. Seymour in "Induced subgraph density. V. All paths approach Erdos-Hajnal(2024)" In conclusion, the paper proves that $P_5$ satisfy this conjecture. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

a variant of this question which is also open is to even show: there exists a $c=c(H)>0$ and a function $f=f_H$ with $f(n)\to \infty$ as $n\to \infty$. so that every $H$-free graph $G$ on $n$ vertices either has a complete graph of size $n^c$ or an independent set of size $f(n)$. its ridiculous!
